Epic Games刚刚赢得了针对苹果的临时限制令--至少在一定程度上是这样。立即生效，苹果不能通过终止公司的苹果开发者账户或限制苹果平台上的开发者使用Epic虚幻引擎来对Epic Games进行报复。
但在同一项裁决中，法官伊冯娜·冈萨雷斯·罗杰斯(Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers)决定，苹果不会被要求将堡垒之夜带回应用商店-在Etic违反苹果规定增加了应用内支付系统后，苹果已经禁止了微博。
法官辩称，Etic“战略性地选择了违反其与苹果的协议”。
“法院认为，就Epic Games就其游戏(包括堡垒之夜)提出的动议而言，Epic Games尚未表现出不可弥补的损害。目前的困境似乎是它自己造成的，“罗杰斯写道，他认为Epicc”战略性地选择了违反与苹果的协议“，从而扰乱了现状。
但是，罗杰斯认为，维持现状也是她裁定苹果现在不能切断对虚幻引擎的访问的原因。在那里，正是苹果“选择了严厉的行动”，通过威胁虚幻引擎，影响了第三方应用程序开发商，也影响了Etic的声誉。
罗杰斯写道：“Epic Games和苹果可以自由地相互提起诉讼，但他们的纠纷不应对旁观者造成严重破坏。”
“Epic Games和苹果可以自由地相互提起诉讼，但他们的纠纷不应对旁观者造成严重破坏。”
罗杰斯同意Etic的观点，即“对虚幻引擎平台本身以及整个游戏行业都存在潜在的重大损害”，并表示苹果将很难辩称，如果虚幻引擎开发者放弃他们的项目，因为Etic不再能在苹果平台上支持他们，Etic将不会受到伤害。
在周一的听证会上，在裁决之前，罗杰斯法官已经表示，她更受虚幻引擎面临的明显威胁的影响。她在听证会开始时说：“我不倾向于对奥运会给予救济，但我倾向于对虚幻引擎给予救济。”
今天的裁决范围有限，只是为了维持现状，而法院可以听取关于初步禁令的更详细的论点。这项禁令将决定苹果是否可以在审判过程中对堡垒之夜、虚幻引擎或其他各种史诗产品采取行动。预计双方将在未来几周提交辩论，并计划在9月28日就禁令举行全面听证会。
法院还没有决定何时听取关于Etic索赔是非曲直的辩论，但不太可能是今年。当被问及何时准备开始审判时，Etic的律师说他们可能会在4到6个月内准备好；苹果公司建议延长发现期，审判将在10个月内开始。
note_en:
Epic Games just won a  temporary restraining order against Apple — at least in part. Effective immediately, Apple can’t retaliate against Epic Games by terminating the company’s Apple developer accounts or restricting use of Epic’s Unreal Engine by developers on Apple platforms.
 But in the same ruling, Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers decided that Apple will not be required to bring  Fortnite — which  it had banned after Epic added  an in-app payment system in violation of Apple’s rules — back to the App Store.
 The judge argued that Epic “strategically chose to breach its agreements with Apple”
 “The Court finds that with respect to Epic Games’ motion as to its games, including  Fortnite, Epic Games has not yet demonstrated irreparable harm. The current predicament appears of its own making,” Rogers wrote, arguing that Epic “strategically chose to breach its agreements with Apple” and thus disturb the status quo.
 But, Rogers argued, maintaining that status quo is also why she’s ruling that Apple can’t cut off access to the Unreal Engine right now. There, it was Apple who “has chosen to act severely” by impacting both third-party app developers, as well as Epic’s reputation, by threatening the Unreal Engine.
 “Epic Games and Apple are at liberty to litigate against each other, but their dispute should not create havoc to bystanders,” Rogers wrote.
  “Epic Games and Apple are at liberty to litigate against each other, but their dispute should not create havoc to bystanders.”
 Rogers agrees with Epic that there is “potential significant damage to both the Unreal Engine platform itself, and to the gaming industry generally,” and suggested that Apple would have a hard time arguing that Epic won’t be harmed if Unreal Engine developers abandon their projects because Epic can no longer support them on Apple platforms.
 In a hearing on Monday, ahead of the ruling, Judge Rogers had already indicated that she was more swayed by the apparent threat to the Unreal Engine. “I am not inclined to grant relief with respect to the games,” she said at the opening of the hearing, “but I am inclined to grant relief with respect to the Unreal Engine.”
 Today’s ruling is limited in scope, meant only to preserve the status quo while the court can hear more detailed arguments concerning a preliminary injunction. That injunction would determine whether Apple can take action against  Fortnite, the Unreal Engine, or various other Epic products over the course of the trial. The two parties are expected to file their arguments in the coming weeks, with a full hearing on the injunction scheduled for September 28th.
 The court hasn’t settled when it will hear arguments on the merits of Epic’s claims, but it is unlikely to be this year. Asked when they would be prepared to begin trial, Epic’s counsel said they could be prepared within four to six months; Apple suggested a longer discovery period, with the trial beginning in ten months.

手机芯片设计公司ARM Ltd.取消了将两项业务剥离给母公司软银集团(SoftBank Group)的计划，这家日本投资公司正在评估四年前以320亿美元收购的这家半导体公司的选择。
ARM总部设在英国，设计为世界上大多数智能手机供电的电路。一个月前，该公司表示，计划将两项物联网业务转移给软银。
周一，该公司发言人表示，ARM在进一步调查了自己的选择后，决定将这些业务保留在自己的保护伞下。但这位发言人说，该公司将把它们与ARM的核心芯片设计业务区分开来，分开运营和核算，但没有详细说明。
这位发言人说，ARM确定，通过将业务保留在内部，这些业务可以实现剥离给软银的同样好处。
他拒绝透露此举是否或如何与ARM的潜在出售或公开募股有关，总部位于日本的软银一直在考虑这一点。将资产保持在ARM的公司结构下可能会使公司更有价值-或许对潜在买家更具吸引力。
在维权投资者埃利奥特管理公司(Elliott Management Corp.)的压力下，软银近几个月承诺进行约425亿美元的资产处置，几乎所有的资产处置都已经完成或同意。它拥有广泛的持股组合，其中包括在中国电商巨头阿里巴巴集团控股公司(Alibaba Group Holding)和ARM投资公司的大量股份。
ARM为包括苹果(Apple)和三星电子(Samsung Electronic s Co.)在内的客户设计微处理器，但不生产用于智能手机和其他设备的微处理器。它将蓝图授权给高通公司(Qualcomm Inc.)等芯片制造商。
总部位于加州库比蒂诺的苹果公司(Apple Corp.)6月份表示，该公司正在将Mac电脑从英特尔公司(Intel Corp.)的芯片转移到基于ARM设计的内部开发的芯片上。
当软银收购ARM时，在一定程度上是对未来的押注，在未来，几乎所有的设备，如汽车和家用电器，都将连接到互联网。但该公司一直难以在驱动物联网的软件市场找到立足点。
ARM曾寻求剥离的业务包括一个名为Formure Data的部门，该部门为客户提供从物联网设备收集和分析数据的工具，以及第二个专注于管理设备并将其安全连接到网络的IoT平台。
ARM当时表示，此举将使该公司能够专注于其核心半导体设计业务，尽管它仍将与剥离出来的实体合作。
ARM是物联网设备芯片的主要设计商。这些活动不会受到其围绕宝藏数据和物联网平台的举动的影响。
在维权投资者埃利奥特管理公司(Elliott Management Corp.)的压力下，软银近几个月来几乎出售或同意出售其承诺的425亿美元资产处置的全部。它拥有广泛的投资组合，其中包括中国电子商务巨头阿里巴巴集团控股有限公司(Alibaba Group Holding Ltd.)的大量股份和ARM投资。
note_en:
Mobile phone chip designer Arm Ltd. has called off a planned spinoff of two businesses to parent company  SoftBank Group    9984  2.44%  as the Japanese investment firm evaluates options for the semiconductor company it  bought four years ago for $32 billion.
  Arm is based in the U.K. and designs circuitry that powers the majority of the world’s smartphones. A month ago, the company said it planned to transfer two Internet of Things businesses to SoftBank.
  On Monday, a company spokesman said that Arm, after further investigating its options, decided to keep the businesses under its umbrella. But the company will keep them distinct from Arm’s core chip-design business, with separate operations and accounting, the spokesman said, without elaborating.
  Arm determined that the businesses could realize the same benefits of a spinoff to SoftBank by keeping the operations in-house, the spokesman said.
      He declined to say if or how the move related to the  potential sale or public offering of Arm, which Japan-based SoftBank has been considering. Keeping the assets under Arm’s corporate structure could make the company more valuable—and perhaps more attractive to potential buyers.
  Under pressure for change from activist investor Elliott Management Corp., SoftBank in recent months promised some $42.5 billion  in asset disposals, almost all of which it has completed or agreed to. It has a broad portfolio of holdings that include a large stake in China’s e-commerce giant  Alibaba Group Holding  and the Arm investment.
  Arm designs but doesn’t manufacture microprocessors for customers that include  Apple  and  Samsung Electronic s Co. that go into smartphones and other devices. It licenses the blueprints to chip makers such as  Qualcomm  Inc.
  Apple is  moving its Mac computers from  Intel  Corp. chips to internally developed ones based on Arm designs, the Cupertino, Calif.-based company said in June.
  When SoftBank bought Arm, it was partly a bet on a future where almost every device, such as cars and household appliances, would be connected to the internet. But the company has  struggled to find a foothold in the market for software that drives the Internet of Things.
  The businesses that Arm had looked to spin off include a unit called Treasure Data, which offers tools for customers to harvest and analyze data from Internet of Things devices, and a second called IoT Platform that focuses on managing devices and securely connecting them to networks.
  The move, Arm said at the time, would allow the company to focus on its core semiconductor design business, although it would still collaborate with the spun-off entities.
    Arm is a major designer of chips that go into Internet of Things devices. Those activities aren’t affected by its moves around Treasure Data and IoT Platform.
  Under pressure for change from activist investor Elliott Management Corp., SoftBank, in recent months has sold or agreed to sell almost all of the $42.5 billion in asset disposals it promised. It has a broad portfolio of holdings that include a large stake in China’s e-commerce giant Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. and the Arm investment.

title_en: Arm says it has called off a spinoff of two IoT businesses to SoftBank that it announced a month ago, but will keep them distinct from Arm's core chip business

三星的Find My Mobile应用程序旨在帮助您远程定位设备、将数据备份到Samsung Cloud、删除本地数据，以及在丢失或被盗时阻止对Samsung Pay的访问。然而，这款应用程序需要正常的网络连接才能执行上述所有功能。这意味着，如果你的设备失去了网络覆盖，你就没有办法使用这款应用程序定位它。值得庆幸的是，三星现在正在推出Find My Mobile应用程序的更新，以解决这个问题。
Find My Mobile应用程序(7.2.05.44版)的最新更新增加了一项新的“离线查找”功能，可以让你使用别人的Galaxy设备找到你的手机，即使你的手机没有连接到网络。该功能还将允许其他用户使用您的手机扫描可能在附近丢失的Galaxy设备。此外，如果Galaxy手表和耳机已连接到您的设备，该功能还可以让您找到它们。
让我们通过使用其他Galaxy用户来跟踪您的手机，即使它没有WiFi或手机。Pic.twitter.com/psLl1rcb4X。
我们团队的马克斯·温巴赫(Max Weinbach)最近发现了这一功能，他分享了上面的截图。正如你在屏幕截图中看到的那样，一旦你在三星Galaxy设备上收到最新的Find My Mobile更新，你的手机就会显示一条新功能的通知。点击通知会立即打开相应的设置页面，在这里你可以通过点击右上角的开关来启用这一功能。您还可以从相同的设置页面加密您的脱机位置。打开该功能后，即使手机未连接到网络，您也可以找到它。

note_en:
Samsung’s  Find My Mobile app is designed to help you remotely locate your device, back up data to Samsung Cloud, delete local data, and block access to  Samsung Pay in case of loss or theft. However, the app requires a working network connection to perform all of the aforementioned functions. This means that if your device loses network coverage, there’s no way for you to locate it using the app. Thankfully, Samsung is now rolling out an update for the Find My Mobile app which addresses this issue.
 The latest update for the Find My Mobile app (version 7.2.05.44) adds a new ‘Offline finding’ feature that will let you find your phone using someone else’s Galaxy device, even when your device isn’t connected to a network. The feature will also let other users use your phone to scan for lost Galaxy devices that may be nearby. Additionally, the feature will let you find Galaxy Watches and earbuds if they were connected to your device.
 Let’s you track your phone even if it doesn’t have WiFi or cellular by using other Galaxy users.  pic.twitter.com/psLl1rcb4X
  The feature was recently spotted by   Max Weinbach from our team, who shared the above screenshots. As you can see in the screenshots, your phone will display a notification for the new feature as soon as you receive the latest Find My Mobile update on your Samsung Galaxy device. Tapping on the notification will instantly open up the respective settings page, where you’ll be able to enable the feature by tapping on the toggle in the top right corner. You’ll also be able to encrypt your offline location from the same settings page. Once the feature is turned on, you’ll be able to find your phone even if it’s not connected to a network.
 While we can’t confirm how this feature works just yet, it appears that it’s only available in the U.S. and South Korea,  according to one user who dug through the SmartThings app.
 You can download version 7.2.05.44 of the Find My Mobile app from the  Samsung Galaxy Store or from  APKMirror. Once we confirm how this feature works, we’ll update this article with those details.
 This article was updated at 6:38 PM EST on August 24, 2020, to correct the version number of the Find My Mobile app that adds this new functionality. We also added details on the country availability. Thanks to AP’s Ryne Hager for the heads up!

note_en:
TikTok  has been or  is in the process of being banned in some countries. This has resulted in new or existing services trying to replicate its success, while Google Discover is now directly highlighting “Short videos.”
  Discover appears to the left of most Android homescreens or in the Google app, which is also available on iOS. It’s a feed mostly comprised of articles related to your interests, while YouTube videos, the weather, and sports scores also appear.  Reaching hundreds of millions of users every month, Discover also shows AMP content.
 Google is now also surfacing a carousel of “Short videos.” This is  distinctly different from the one for Accelerated Mobile Pages “Stories” that we spotted in June.
 The example seen today is populated with content from Indian TikTok competitor  Trell, as well as Google’s Area 120 project  Tangi. The latter service is home to 60-second DIY “ quick videos.”
  A logo appears in the top-left corner, while the video name appears at the bottom. You can hide any clip from the overflow menu.
 These Short videos open in your device’s default web browser. In the case of Trell content, it’s an AMP link complete with another logo in the corner and “Click here” button to see more content. There are also controls to un/mute and share, as well as a progress indicator. The content from Tangi is not an AMP link but has similar branding and player UI.
 So far, we’ve only seen one report of Short videos in Google Discover. It’s not clear how widely rolled out this carousel is, nor what criteria Google is using to highlight content. YouTube is rumored to be working on its own TikTok competitor called “ Shorts” for later this year. That will presumably also appear in the new Discover carousel.

note:
据知情人士透露，通用大西洋(General Atlantic)和红杉资本(Sequoia Capital)是TikTok中国母公司的两个主要投资者，它们正在努力成为收购这家广受欢迎的视频分享应用程序的美国业务的交易的一部分，因为TikTok寻求避免特朗普政府的禁令。
知情人士说，这些投资公司拥有北京ByteDance Ltd.的大量股份，是包括甲骨文(Oracle)在内的一个集团可能竞购TikTok的关键推动因素。甲骨文集团最近成为微软的可能替代品，微软本月早些时候表示，正在就收购TikTok在美国和其他三个国家的业务进行谈判。
微软曾表示，可能会邀请一些美国投资者加入其竞购。但一些知情人士说，最近红杉资本(Sequoia)和通用大西洋公司(General Atlantic)越来越担心自己在微软的交易中没有一席之地，并寻找另一个潜在的技术合作伙伴，可以让他们分一杯羹。他们现在正在推动潜在的甲骨文竞购，这很快赢得了特朗普总统的公开支持，尽管一些人士表示，微软的谈判是变化无常的，外部投资者仍可能被纳入微软竞购的少数股权投资者。
红杉(Sequoia)和通用大西洋(General Atlantic)都是ByteDance董事会的成员。红杉资本的席位由其中国区负责人沈南鹏(Neil Shenin)担任，而其在美国参与收购的努力则由全球执行合伙人道格·里昂(Doug Leone)领导。General Atlantic的努力由其首席执行官比尔·福特(Bill Ford)领导，他在ByteDance董事会代表自己的公司。投资公司有两点潜在的利益冲突：作为董事会成员，他们有义务最大化ByteDance宝贵资产的价值，以及试图以好价格收购美国的TikTok，以利用其潜力。
ByteDance一直面临压力，要求其在白宫8月6日的行政命令中设定的45天最后期限之前达成协议，出售TikTok的美国分支。根据该命令，如果TikTok不出售给美国买家，ByteDance就会被禁止。特朗普政府表示，这款应用对美国的利益构成了经济和国家安全威胁。随后的总统命令为完成任何交易设定了90天的最后期限。
知情人士称，竞购者已被要求在本周末前提交报价，其中一方可能很快进入排他性谈判。然而，目前还不清楚是否会发生出售。TikTok周一提起诉讼，对特朗普最初的行政命令提出质疑，称他的政府在发布行政命令时没有遵循正当程序。
至少有三个阵营一直在考虑可能的TikTok交易。知情人士说，除了微软和甲骨文集团，Twitter还就潜在的合并进行了初步谈判。
知情人士说，Twitter仍有兴趣，但其接洽并未取得重大进展。
一些知情人士说，微软的讨论比任何其他谈判都要深入，考虑到紧迫的时间表和复杂的谈判，这可能是一个关键优势，特别是在涉及到保证两家公司能够满足美国国家安全担忧的情况下。微软表示，其目标是不迟于9月1日完成与ByteDance的谈判。15，并补充说，它是否能够达成交易并不确定。
TikTok的代表已经与其他公司就可能参与竞购一事进行了接触。知情人士说，他们与Netflix接洽，以评估其对交易的兴趣，但其中一名知情人士说，这家视频流媒体巨头通过了。
甲骨文并不像微软那样是一个明显的潜在买家。甲骨文拥有相当大的云计算运营和技术能力，但它主要专注于服务商业客户，几乎没有运营社交媒体平台或其他主要面向消费者的业务的经验。微软在商业计算领域是一个更大的参与者，但也拥有LinkedIn社交媒体网站和Xbox视频游戏业务。它的规模也要大得多，拥有约1360亿美元的现金，大约是甲骨文手头现金的三倍。
由于ByteDance要么必须同意让政府满意的交易，要么冒着其TikTok资产在美国被完全禁止的风险，微软和其他竞购者在价格谈判中拥有重要的筹码。
人们对估值的看法大相径庭。其中一位知情人士说，ByteDance估计TikTok在美国和其他国家的业务合计价值可能超过500亿美元，而一些熟悉TikTok业务的人士认为这个数字要低得多。TikTok在美国拥有约1亿用户，他们被认为是这款应用程序在全球范围内最有利可图的用户之一，据信这款应用程序仍在亏损。
支付贱卖价格可能会给在中国拥有可观业务的微软或其他买家带来风险，因为这可能会进一步激起中国的情绪。在中国，有关被迫出售和美国政府从交易中提成的言论一直受到批评。
了解字节跳动想法的人士称，字节跳动创始人兼首席执行官张一鸣青睐微软。其中一位知情人士说，曾在该公司短暂工作过的张喜欢该公司的文化，并与微软首席执行长纳德拉(Satya Nadella)关系良好。
虽然甲骨文缺乏运营消费科技业务的经验，但它拥有政治人脉，这可能有助于其对TikTok的追求。今年早些时候，甲骨文的联合创始人、董事长和最大股东拉里·埃里森(Larry Ellison)在他家为总统举办了一场筹款活动。首席执行官萨夫拉·卡茨(Safra Catz)在2016年也曾在特朗普过渡团队的执行委员会工作，并为他的连任竞选活动捐款。
上周，当被问及甲骨文可能的竞购时，特朗普说，“嗯，我认为甲骨文是一家伟大的公司，我认为它的所有者是一个了不起的人，一个了不起的人。我认为甲骨文肯定是能处理好这件事的人。“。
红杉资本也与美国政府有联系，并一直在游说如何让TikTok继续在美国运营。据知情人士透露，红杉资本全球执行合伙人里昂已致电财政部长史蒂文·姆钦(Steven Mnuchin)和特朗普的顾问兼女婿贾里德·库什纳(Jared Kushner)，作为这一努力的一部分。
在这个选举周期里，里昂和他的妻子已经向共和党候选人捐赠了数万美元，包括为总统连任所做的努力。今年1月，里昂在国务卿迈克·庞皮欧(Mike Pompeo)位于加利福尼亚州阿瑟顿的家中为他举行了招待会。
福特在这个选举周期也向共和党国会候选人捐赠了数千美元。
写信给罗尔夫·温克勒(Rolfe.winkler@wsj.com)、米里亚姆·戈特弗里德(Miriam.Gottred@wsj.com)和卡拉·隆巴多(Cara Lombardo)，电子邮件是rolfe.winkler@wsj.com，电子邮件是cara.lombardo@wsj.com
note_en:
General Atlantic and Sequoia Capital, two major investors in TikTok’s Chinese parent company, are maneuvering to be part of a deal to acquire the U.S. operations of the popular video-sharing app as it seeks to avoid a ban by the Trump administration, according to people familiar with the discussions.
  The investment firms, which own large stakes in Beijing-based ByteDance Ltd., are key drivers behind a possible bid for TikTok by a group including  Oracle  the people said. The Oracle group  emerged recently as a possible alternative to  Microsoft  which  said early this month that it was in talks to buy TikTok’s operations in the U.S. and three other countries.
  Microsoft had said it might invite some U.S. investors to join its bid. But more recently Sequoia and General Atlantic grew concerned that they wouldn’t have a place in a Microsoft deal and looked for another potential tech partner that could give them a piece of the action, some of the people said. They are now pushing the potential Oracle bid, which quickly won President Trump’s public support, although some of the people said the Microsoft talks are fluid and outside investors could still be included as minority investors in Microsoft’s bid.
  Sequoia and General Atlantic both hold seats on ByteDance’s board. Sequoia’s seat is occupied by its China head, Neil Shen, while its efforts in the U.S. to participate in an acquisition are being led by Global Managing Partner Doug Leone. General Atlantic’s effort is led by its chief executive, Bill Ford, who represents his firm on ByteDance’s board. The investment firms have two points of interest that potentially conflict: their obligation as board members to maximize the value to ByteDance of its prized asset, and trying to buy into TikTok in the U.S. at a good price to capitalize on its potential.
    ByteDance has been  under pressure to reach a deal to sell TikTok’s U.S. arm ahead of a 45-day deadline the White House imposed in an Aug. 6 executive order that bans the app—which the Trump administration says poses an economic and national-security threat to U.S. interests—if it isn’t sold to U.S. buyers. A subsequent presidential order  set a 90-day deadline for any transaction to be completed.
    Bidders have been asked to submit offers by the end of the week, and one of the parties could enter exclusive negotiations soon, according to people familiar with the matter. It isn’t clear that a sale will happen, however. TikTok  filed a lawsuit Monday challenging Mr. Trump’s initial executive order, saying his administration failed to follow due process in issuing it.
  At least three camps have been circling a possible TikTok deal. In addition to Microsoft and the Oracle group,  Twitter  also  had preliminary talks about a potential combination, people familiar with those discussions said.
  Twitter remains interested but its approach hasn’t made significant headway, the people said.
  Microsoft’s discussions are more advanced than any others, some of the people familiar with talks said—potentially a critical advantage given the tight timetable and the complexity of the negotiations, especially as it relates to guaranteeing the companies could satisfy U.S. national-security concerns. Microsoft has said it aimed to complete discussions with ByteDance no later than Sept. 15, adding there is no certainty it would be able to conclude a deal.
  TikTok representatives have engaged with other companies about potential involvement in a bid. They approached  Netflix  to gauge its interest in a deal, people familiar with those discussions said, but the video-streaming giant passed, one of them said.
  Oracle isn’t as obvious a potential buyer as Microsoft. Oracle has a sizable cloud-computing operation and technical capabilities, but it is mainly focused on serving business clients and has virtually no experience running a social-media platform or other major consumer-facing business. Microsoft is an even bigger player in business computing but also owns the LinkedIn social-media site and the Xbox videogame business. It also is much bigger and has about $136 billion of cash, some three times what Oracle has on hand.
  Because ByteDance either has to agree to a deal that satisfies the government or risk its TikTok asset being banned entirely in the U.S., Microsoft and other suitors have significant leverage in price negotiations.
  There are widely different views on valuation. ByteDance has estimated TikTok’s U.S. operations and those in the other countries could collectively be worth more than $50 billion, one of the people said, while some people familiar with TikTok’s operations put the number significantly lower. TikTok has around 100 million users in the U.S., and they are considered among the most lucrative world-wide for the app, which is still believed to be losing money.
  Paying a fire-sale price could pose a risk for Microsoft, which has a sizable business in China, or another buyer, by  potentially further inflaming sentiment in China, where talk of a forced sale and the U.S. government taking a cut from the transaction has been criticized.
  Zhang Yiming, ByteDance’s founder and chief executive, favors Microsoft, said people familiar with his thinking. Mr. Zhang, who briefly worked at the company, likes its culture and has a good relationship with Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella, one of these people said.
  While Oracle lacks experience running consumer tech businesses, it has political connections that could help its pursuit of TikTok. Larry Ellison, Oracle’s co-founder, chairman and largest shareholder, earlier this year  threw a fundraiser at his home for the president. Chief Executive Safra Catz also  worked on the executive committee for the Trump transition team in 2016 and has donated to his re-election campaign.
  Asked about a possible Oracle bid last week, Mr. Trump said, “Well, I think Oracle is a great company and I think its owner is a tremendous guy, a tremendous person. I think that Oracle would be certainly somebody that could handle it.”
  Sequoia also has ties to the administration and has been  lobbying for ways to enable TikTok to keep operating in the U.S. Mr. Leone, the Sequoia global managing partner, has called Treasury Secretary Steven Mnuchin and Mr. Trump’s adviser and son-in-law Jared Kushner as part of this effort, according to people familiar with those discussions.
  Mr. Leone and his wife have donated tens of thousands of dollars to Republican candidates this election cycle, including to the president’s re-election effort. In January, Mr. Leone hosted a reception for Secretary of State Mike Pompeo at his Atherton, Calif., home.
  Mr. Ford has donated thousands of dollars to Republican congressional candidates this election cycle as well.
    Write to Rolfe Winkler at  rolfe.winkler@wsj.com, Miriam Gottfried at  Miriam.Gottfried@wsj.com and Cara Lombardo at  cara.lombardo@wsj.com
title: 消息来源：ByteDance的两大投资者General Atlantic和红杉资本(Sequoia Capital)是甲骨文竞购TikTok的关键推动因素；Twitter的竞购进展甚微
title_en: Sources: General Atlantic and Sequoia Capital, two top investors in ByteDance, are key drivers in Oracle's bid for TikTok; Twitter's bid has made little headway
